description: selene is installed and selene.toml config is in place
steps: Assert `selene.toml` references the vim standard library
Run selene on a clean Lua file — assert exit 0
Run selene on a file with a known violation — assert exit 1

description: lua-lint-selene reports a violation on a file with a known selene error
steps: Create a temporary Lua file containing a known selene violation (e.g. undefined global usage)
Assert output contains a line matching the ▮-separated format with the correct code

description: lua-lint-selene exits 0 with no output on a clean Lua file
steps: Create a temporary Lua file with no selene violations

description: rule-no-vim-deepcopy flags a line containing vim.deepcopy(
steps: Create a temporary Lua file containing `local x = vim.deepcopy(t)` on a known line number
Assert output contains a violation line with code `noVimDeepcopy` at the correct line number

description: rule-no-vim-deepcopy produces no output on a file without vim.deepcopy
steps: Create a temporary Lua file with no calls to vim.deepcopy

description: lua-lint merges violations from both selene and custom rules
steps: Create a temporary Lua file containing both a selene violation and a vim.deepcopy( call
Assert JSON output contains at least two violation objects
Assert one object has a selene-originated code and one has noVimDeepcopy

description: lua-lint outputs empty JSON array and exits 0 on a clean file
steps: Create a temporary Lua file with no selene violations and no custom rule violations

description: lua-lint exits 1 when only custom violations are present
steps: Create a temporary Lua file with only a vim.deepcopy( call (no selene violations)
Assert JSON output contains exactly one violation with code noVimDeepcopy

description: lua-lint violations appear as diagnostics in Neovim (HITL)
steps: Open a Lua file containing vim.deepcopy( in Neovim
Assert the noVimDeepcopy violation appears as an inline diagnostic
Assert lua_ls completion and go-to-definition still work
Assert stylua formatting on save still works
Assert no spurious duplicate diagnostics are shown

description: committing a Lua file with a violation is blocked by the pre-commit hook
steps: Stage a Lua file containing a vim.deepcopy( call

description: CLAUDE.md documents lua-lint and git-file-lint handles Lua files
steps: Run `git-file-lint` in a repo with a dirty Lua file — assert violations are reported
Run `git-file-lint` with no dirty Lua files — assert no error

description: stylua is installed and works from the terminal
lua-fix formats a single file in place
steps: Assert output matches what Neovim conform produces on save

description: lua-fix formats multiple files in one invocation
steps: Assert both files pass `stylua --check` after formatting

description: committing a staged Lua file auto-applies stylua via lint-staged
steps: Assert the committed file is stylua-clean

description: CLAUDE.md documents lua-fix and lint-staged runs both lua-lint and lua-fix
steps: Assert lint-staged Lua pattern runs both lua-lint and lua-fix

description: lua-test-path returns the path when given an existing _spec.lua file
steps: Assert output is the path to `foo_spec.lua`

description: lua-test-path exits 1 when given a _spec.lua path that does not exist
lua-test-path resolves source file to its spec when spec exists
steps: Create a temporary directory with `lodash.lua` and `__tests__/lodash_spec.lua`

description: lua-test-path exits 1 silently when no spec exists for a source file
steps: Create a temporary directory with `lodash.lua` and no `__tests__/` directory

description: lua-test runs a passing spec and exits 0
steps: Assert output contains the mini.test pass indicator

description: lua-test resolves a source file to its spec and runs it
steps: Assert exit code is 0 (spec exists and passes)

description: lua-test exits 0 silently when called on a file with no spec
steps: Run `lua-test config/vim/nvim/config/functions/hacks.lua` (no spec exists for this file)

description: committing a staged Lua file with a failing spec blocks the commit
steps: Stage a Lua file whose spec has a failing test

description: CLAUDE.md documents lua-test and git-file-test handles Lua files
steps: Run `git-file-test` in a repo with a dirty Lua file that has a spec — assert spec runs
Run `git-file-test` with no dirty Lua files — assert no error
